paragraphs.text: I am 30 years old and this is the first time, in my whole life, where I felt that something could have been achieved by institutions thanks to people that care about preserving art and respecting the rights of customers. It's art, videogames are art, there is time, effort, sweat, long days and stressful nights of people working behind them and once again the threat of big, rich companies taking over institutions is real and the European Union, the forge of art, the cauldron of culture, can do something, it may be something small but it could be seen and remembered by future generation as the only union of people joining forces against cultural and artistic destruction, no matter its form, whether it's digital or physical, the work of people, paid by people must remain to the people, no one could have the right to take away something that is ours, something that one day I could be playing with my grandchildrens, making them feel like I was feeling looking at those old yet brilliant movies in black and white. Don't let them do that, please, show the world that there is still someone that goes beyond the mere value of money and cares about the people. It's a simple thing we are asking for, we just fear what we love to be taken away from us for no reason at all, if not to push people to buy more and more, taking away value from any creation made by human effort. Big companies like VISA and Mastercard, other among the big giants of the world, are threatening as well the freedom of videogames, the freedom of expression we reached after all the people before us, that fought one way or another to reach this point. We need protection, be the hero we all wish for.
